• Prepare recommendation for findings/problems found at the client’s facilities during servicing, submit to Operations Head/Acting GM for approval

• Preparation of Purchase Requisition (PR) for materials, components, parts, equipment and supplies for each WO/Project, making sure that correct technical specification is given/written with supporting data, pictures, drawing, data and all relevant information are attached to PR

• Resolution of all problems or dispute in all Services and Installation projects that may arise before, during and after implementation. It is the sole responsibility of the SE to ensure that said problems or dispute are fully resolved to the satisfaction of the clients/ customer and PSSI. Management

• Technical assistance shall be given to purchasing all the time as needed by purchasing to ensure that the requested items are purchased as specified at the best possible price and on-time as needed by the project

• Submit a copy of the report to sales and marketing department for proposal/quotation preparation

• Review submitted test reports from the subcontractors

• Maintain a professional appearance and manner at all times, consistent with client and company expectations and in doing so help to promote the image of the Company

• Set a good example to colleagues by observing and obeying all company rules and regulations, complying with all site regulations, and safeguarding Company properties

• Observe and adhere to company health & safety guidelines and contribute to a healthy and safe working environment

• Ensure that guidelines and standards of work expected by PSSI are understood and met

• Ensure to complete all necessary Company documentation punctually and in accordance with any guidelines or schedules issued (including Time Sheets, Staff Expenses, site reports, etc.)

• Reporting and communications protocol at office (Reporting to Acting Operations Head/GM)

a. Stand-up reporting daily – report daily to GM all plan activities for the day

b. Call and text reporting before, during and after completion of services

c. Formal (written) report that will be submitted to the client

• Reporting and communications protocol with Customers’ Representative

a. Upon arrival at site – ensure that contact Engineer will be contacted for coordination on site.

b. During work in progress – any queries will be coordinated to customer’s representative

• c. After completion of work – ensure all checklist are signed, replaced materials are properly turned over and signed by customer’s representative, and ensure tool keeper/s have checked tools and equipment prior to departure from site.

• Prepare training modules/methodology for test equipment to use in training of existing and new technicians.

• Schedule training and conduct training for all test equipment available in PSSI.

• Identification of training to be prioritized for the operations.

• Generation and preparation of training materials to be initiated.

• Arrangements of training and cooperate with the HRA for the schedule.

title: Landing Page Designer, Conversion Focused
description:
Are you looking for an opportunity to make an incredible impact in the world of socially conscious brands?



Lucyd Media is a social advertising agency, specializing in the compliant advertising for CBD, Cannabis, and other DTC wellness e-commerce brands. Our mission is to reduce human suffering and maximize human potential by facilitating the distribution of Conscious Compounds™



We are currently seeking a strong landing page (LP) designer to lead the design and optimization of the post-click experience across our paid media funnels.



At Lucyd, we believe the post-click experience is one of the biggest underutilized levers in wellness growth. We need someone who can bring real authority to that part of the funnel, move quickly, use AI aggressively, and help us create a more seamless conversion experience across the brands we serve. This role will use AI-assisted tools to rapidly generate and iterate on landing pages, while applying strong design judgment and conversion strategy.



Ready to elevate your career? We're excited to hear from you.



What You Will Be Responsible For:

• Designing and optimizing landing pages, offer pages, and funnel-specific product pages

• Translating ad hooks and offer strategy into conversion-focused page experiences

• Improving what users land on after the click, whether that is a custom landing page, PDP variant, or other post-click experience

• Supporting page testing, iteration, and optimization based on performance

• Identifying where the current post-click experience is creating friction or hurting conversion

• Helping define how landing pages should be requested, built, and improved within Lucyd

• Using AI tools to speed up production and increase design leverage

• Partnering closely with Growth Strategists, Advertising, and Post-Click teams



What We Are Looking For:

• 3+ years of experience in CRO-focused design, landing page design, funnel design, or conversion-oriented eCommerce design.

• A portfolio demonstrating at least 20 DTC/eCommerce landing pages or product pages (PDPs) you have designed.

• Candidates without relevant portfolio examples will not be considered.

• Experience designing for paid media funnels, not just general web design. You know how to align page structure and messaging to the promise made in the ad.

• Comfort using AI tools to generate and iterate on landing page designs quickly, while applying strong design judgment and strategic direction. You know how to use tools to move faster while still applying judgment and taste.

• Exceptional organizational skills, adept at juggling multiple projects while maintaining top-notch productivity and prioritization.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to manage client expectations and maintain strong relationships.

• Experience in DTC wellness supplements, cannabis or other restricted products or adjacent consumer categories is a plus



Success Roadmap:



In 30 Days, You'll:

• Audit the current pages users land on after the click, including landing pages, PDPs, collections, and offer pages.

• Identify the highest-priority opportunities to improve conversion through better post-click experiences.

• Begin improving the process for how landing pages are requested, built, and reviewed.

• Design and launch your first new landing pages for active client accounts.



In 60 Days, You'll:

• Partner closely with Growth Strategists to align page structure with ad hooks, offers, and buyer intent.

• Start identifying patterns in what is driving or hurting conversion across pages.

• Own the design and optimization of post-click experiences for multiple active accounts.

• Contribute to a faster, more repeatable landing page production process



In 90 Days, You'll:

• Be the clear internal owner of conversion-focused post-click design

• Establish a more seamless system for building, iterating, and optimizing landing pages.

• Consistently use AI tools to increase speed, output, and design leverage.

• Provide strong guidance to the team on what users should land on after they click on an ad.

 

By Your One-Year Anniversary:

You will have helped establish Lucyd’s post-click experience as a meaningful performance lever across paid media. You will have contributed to:

• Faster landing page creation and iteration

• Higher-quality post-click experiences

• Better alignment between ads, offers, and page structure

• A more intentional system for designing and optimizing pages users land on after the click



Apply now to become an integral part of our mission to connect socially conscious brands with their audiences in meaningful ways.

title: WordPress / Elementor Website Support Specialist
description:
We are looking for a skilled, detail-oriented WordPress / Elementor Website Support Specialist to help maintain and improve our websites.



This is not a general virtual assistant role. We already have support for creative, editing, and administrative work. This role is specifically for someone who is confident in WordPress, Elementor, page building, mobile responsiveness, layout cleanup, and SEO-conscious website implementation.



You will help support multiple websites related to leadership coaching, training, workshops, and podcast content. In most cases, the strategy, copy, SEO direction, and content guidance will already be provided. We need someone who can take that guidance and turn it into clean, polished, professional, SEO-friendly pages.



Core responsibilities include:



Building and updating pages in WordPress using Elementor

Implementing new copy based on provided guidance while preserving readability and page structure

Formatting and publishing blog posts, landing pages, service pages, and podcast pages

Applying on-page SEO best practices using Yoast

Improving layout, spacing, heading structure, and visual consistency across pages

Making pages responsive for tablet and mobile

Recreating or refining sections based on mockups, references, or written direction

Supporting reusable templates and section systems in Elementor

Handling light CSS adjustments when needed

Troubleshooting formatting issues, plugin conflicts, and display inconsistencies



We are looking for someone who:



Has strong hands-on WordPress experience

Has strong Elementor experience

Understands on-page SEO fundamentals

Is comfortable working inside Yoast

Can implement SEO updates carefully without breaking page design or user experience

Has a good eye for spacing, hierarchy, and clean modern layouts

Can follow an existing brand style

Works carefully and communicates clearly

Notices details without needing constant supervision



Important for this role:

We already have a roadmap of SEO improvements to implement across the site. This person should be comfortable updating pages based on SEO guidance, including headings, internal links, metadata inputs, image alt text, page structure, and content formatting.



Bonus points for experience with:



Yoast Premium

internal linking best practices

schema-related plugin fields

website speed and performance best practices

staging sites and safe update workflows

service-based or content-driven websites



Hours: approximately 10-20 hours per week, with room to grow.



To apply, please send:



A short introduction about your WordPress and Elementor experience

3–5 examples of pages or websites you have personally built or managed in Elementor

1–2 examples of SEO-related page updates you have implemented

Your hourly rate

Your weekly availability

The word BLUEPRINT in your application, so we know you read the full post



Please also answer:



What kinds of Elementor work do you do best?

How do you make sure a page looks good on mobile?

How do you usually optimize a page for SEO when guidance is provided?

Have you worked with Yoast before?

Have you worked with reusable templates or global styles before?



Finalists may be asked to complete a small paid test project.

title: GUEST COMMUNICATION COORDINATOR — MORNING SHIFT (PART-TIME, REMOTE)
description:
GUEST COMMUNICATION COORDINATOR — MORNING SHIFT (PART-TIME, REMOTE)



About Ariser

Ariser manages 12 serviced apartments in Central Switzerland. We

provide high-quality short-term stays for business travelers and

tourists. Our team is small, efficient, and technology-driven — we use

a PMS, channel manager and own management software, smart locks, and

automated workflows to keep operations lean.



About the Role

We are looking for a proactive, reliable guest communication

specialist to own the morning shift (08:00–14:00 CET, Monday to

Friday). You will be the first point of contact for our guests — on

the phone, via messaging platforms, and in writing.



This is a part-time role with approximately 10–15 effective hours per

week. On a typical morning, you'll handle 2–5 phone calls and 2–5

written messages (guest inquiries, booking changes, coordination with

our on-site cleaning and maintenance team).



Important: We are looking for someone who is already working in

STR/hospitality during these hours and can integrate our properties

into their existing workflow. We pay for effective work time, not

standby. You should be online and responsive during the morning

window, but we understand you may be supporting other clients

simultaneously.



What You'll Do

• Guest Communication (Phone & Written): Answer incoming calls,

respond to guest messages on Airbnb and 
 — from first

inquiry through check-out. Tone: professional, warm,

solution-oriented.

• Problem-Solving: Handle guest issues (smart lock access, heating,

Wi-Fi, noise) with empathy and speed. Escalate only when necessary.

• Operations Coordination: Keep our on-site team (cleaners, handyman)

updated via WhatsApp. Track tasks in Trello. Ensure smooth turnovers

between stays.

• Booking Management: Process reservation changes, cancellations, and

special requests across platforms.



What We're Looking For

• 1+ years of hands-on experience in guest communication for

short-term rentals or hospitality

• Excellent spoken and written English — you will be on the phone with

international guests who need clear, confident communication

• Proactive mindset — you don't wait for instructions. You see what

needs to be done and you do it. You think ahead, flag issues before

they escalate, and suggest improvements.

• Reliability — stable high-speed internet, computer, and mobile

phone. No exceptions.

• Tool proficiency — Airbnb, 
, Google Workspace, WhatsApp,

Trello. You know what a PMS and channel manager are.



Schedule & Compensation

• Hours: 10–15 hours/week (part-time)

• Availability: Monday–Friday, 08:00–14:00 CET

• Rate: Hourly

• Start: Immediately

• Duration: Long-term / ongoing



??????????????????????????????????????



HOW TO APPLY — VOICE SAMPLE REQUIRED



To be considered, please submit ALL of the following:



1. Your CV

2. References from previous hospitality or STR roles

3. A short voice recording (1-2 minutes total)



For the voice recording, please go through these 3 guest scenarios as

if you were actually speaking to a guest on the phone. Speak

naturally, in your own words — just cover the key points of each

scenario.



SCENARIO 1 — WELCOME CALL

A guest calls because they just arrived and can't get the electronic

door lock to open.

? Greet them, ask what they see on the lock display, walk them through

the unlock process (enter code, wait for green light, push handle

down), ask if they need anything else.



SCENARIO 2 — COMPLAINT HANDLING

A guest calls to say the heating isn't working.

? Show empathy, ask which apartment, promise to contact maintenance

within 30 minutes, suggest the extra blanket in the bedroom closet as

a temporary fix.



SCENARIO 3 — OUTBOUND CHECK-IN CALL

You're calling a guest who checked in yesterday to check if everything is okay.

? Introduce yourself, ask if the apartment is comfortable, offer local

recommendations, remind them to reach out anytime.



Format: Voice memo, MP3, or any audio file. Applications without a

voice sample will not be reviewed.



??????????????????????????????????????



We value quality over quantity. If you're someone who takes ownership

and genuinely cares about guest experience, we'd love to hear from

you.

title: Sales Operations Associate
description:
About the Role



We're looking for a detail-oriented Sales Operations Associate to join our revenue team. This is a supporting role that works closely with our Account Managers (based in Europe and Asia) to keep our advertising campaigns running accurately and efficiently.



You'll own the operational side of campaign management — setting up new offers, configuring reward structures, maintaining campaign parameters, and making sure everything is live, correct, and documented. You won't be talking to clients directly; you'll be the engine behind the scenes, freeing up our Account Managers to focus on strategy and relationships.



This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, comfortable in a structured process environment, and wants to build a career in digital advertising operations.



What You'll Do



Set up new advertising campaigns in our internal admin platform based on briefs provided by Account Managers

Configure reward parameters (CPI values, payout structures, milestone settings) following established guidelines

Perform quality assurance checks on new campaign launches before they go live

Make optimization adjustments to live campaigns as directed by Account Managers (e.g., CPI updates, budget changes, geo targeting)

Maintain accurate campaign trackers and status documentation

Flag performance anomalies or setup issues to the relevant Account Manager

Support ad hoc operational requests from the revenue team

What We're Looking For



1–3 years of experience in a digital advertising, ad operations, or campaign management support role

Strong attention to detail — you catch errors before they become problems

Comfortable working in internal tools and dashboards (we use a proprietary admin platform, Looker, and standard project tracking tools)

Reliable async communicator — you can take a written brief and execute without needing a call

English proficiency (written and verbal) — all communication with the team is in English

Ability to work overlap hours with Europe (approx. 2–4 hours daily overlap with CET/GMT+1 is expected)

Nice to Have



Familiarity with mobile advertising metrics (CPI, installs, retention, IAP/IAA)

Experience with rewarded advertising or offer wall platforms

Background in adtech, gaming, or performance marketing

What This Is Not



This is not a client-facing or sales role. You will not be negotiating deals or managing advertiser relationships. This is a precision ops role — the measure of success is accuracy, speed, and consistency.

title: Remote IT Setup — Sync Two Windows Computers (Home + Office)
description:
I need an experienced IT professional to remotely set up and sync two Windows desktop computers for a small business owner.



The Situation:



Two Windows desktop computers in two separate locations (home and office/warehouse)

Both are currently on local accounts — not connected to each other

Files, settings, and work done on one computer do not carry over to the other



What We Need:



Set up one Microsoft account and connect both computers to it

Enable OneDrive so files sync automatically across both machines (set up OneDrive subscription to more storage if necessary)

Configure remote desktop access (TeamViewer or equivalent) so the owner can access his office computer from home when needed

Make sure no existing files are lost during the process

Everything done remotely — no on-site visit required



What You Must Do Before Starting:



Confirm all existing files are backed up and synced before making any changes

Walk me through what you're doing at each step

Leave both computers fully functional at the end of the session



Deliverables:



Both computers synced and are sharing the same files via OneDrive

Remote desktop access working and tested on both ends

Brief summary of what was set up so we can reference it later



Note: If you have another solution, you can walk me through it.



Requirements:



Must be done via remote session (TeamViewer preferred)

Must have verified reviews and completed jobs

Must communicate clearly in English

Must be available within the next few days



Budget: Open to quotes — please message before ordering.



When reaching out, use the job title as your message subject: Remote IT Setup — Sync Two Windows Computers (Home + Office)

About us

---

We are a US-based safety equipment and PPE distribution company that has been in business for 30 years. We sell high-visibility apparel, FR clothing, and pole line hardware to government agencies, oil and gas companies, and industrial clients.

We are building an automated B2B order processing system and need a skilled 
 specialist to help us complete it and maintain it long-term. This is a stable, ongoing role with a company that values reliable, quality work.



What the system does

---

When a customer
s us a Purchase Order (PDF), the automation handles everything:

• Extracts order data from the PDF using AI (PDF.co)

• Validates pricing, quantity, and unit of measure against Shopify

• Creates the order in Shopify

• Checks inventory in inFlow

• Creates a Purchase Order in QuickBooks Online

•
s the supplier and notifies the customer



Tools you must know

---

• 
 — this is the core tool. You must be experienced, not just familiar.

• QuickBooks Online — creating POs, searching vendors and accounts via 

• Shopify — product and inventory queries, ideally GraphQL API

• OpenAI module in 
 — prompting for classification and normalization

• Google Sheets — used for vendor mapping and tracking

• Gmail — automated
 triggers and notifications



What you will work on

---

• Complete the current automation (95% built) — resolve remaining blockers and run end-to-end testing

• Build Scenario 2 — watches for supplier reply
s, classifies them with AI, and routes responses to customers automatically

• Fix bugs and improve reliability as we go live and process real orders

• Work alongside our existing tea
mber who handles day-to-day operations

• Maintain and improve the system over time as the business grows



What we are looking for

---

• Strong 
 experience — complex multi-module scenarios, not simple automations

• Experience connecting 
 to QuickBooks Online API (not just basic integrations)

• Comfortable working with APIs, JSON parsing, variable management, and routers

• Reliable communicator — we need daily updates and clear reporting

• Someone looking for stable, long-term work — not just a quick project

• Can start this week



What we provide

---

• Full access to the existing 
 scenario with detailed documentation (SOP)

• Clear scope and priorities — no guesswork

• A supportive team environment — we are not micromanagers

• Long-term engagement as the business scales

• Consistent hours and on-time payment



How to apply

---

Please send a message that includes:

• Your 
 experience — specifically any builds involving QuickBooks, Shopify, or OpenAI

• A link to your portfolio or 2–3 scenario screenshots showing complex builds

• Your availability and preferred hours per week

• Your rate in USD per hour

We read every application personally. Generic applications without portfolio examples will not be considered. We are looking for someone who takes pride in their work and wants to grow with us.

title: Remote Content, Video & Marketing Specialist
description:
Remote Content, Video & Marketing Specialist — Part-Time (50%)

Your Friend In Reykjavik | 

Who we are

Your Friend In Reykjavik (YFIR) is an award-winning tour operator and destination management company based in Iceland. We run daily walking tours, food tours, folklore and ghost tours, private driving tours, and custom multiday itineraries. We've built over 9,000 five-star reviews across Google, TripAdvisor, GYG, Viator, and Airbnb — not through marketing budgets, but through guides who genuinely love what they do.



We're a small, remote-friendly team spread across multiple countries. We work asynchronously, communicate directly, and don't micromanage.



We're members of Visit Iceland and Visit Reykjavik (Markasstofan hofudborgarsvaedisins), and this hire will work in context and cooperation with both organisations — tapping into their campaigns, content, and marketing calendars as part of a broader Iceland tourism ecosystem.



We're looking for someone to own our content, SEO, and marketing output. This is a role you'll shape.



What you'll do

Content is the engine. Everything else flows from it.



Writing & Content

2-4 SEO-led blog posts per month about Iceland — travel guides, seasonal stories, local culture, hidden gems

Social media captions and content across
, Instagram, LinkedIn, and TikTok —
 is our primary community with 37k+ followers; Instagram and TikTok are active growth targets

Two monthly newsletters via MailerLite — one public, one B2B/partner

WordPress publishing — uploading, formatting, scheduling

We run an active 
munity for travellers visiting Iceland — you'll feed it with content as part of your social media output

Stay aligned with Visit Iceland and Visit Reykjavik marketing calendars and seasonal themes



SEO

Keyword research — identifying what to write based on what people actually search for

On-page SEO — title tags, meta descriptions, heading structure, internal linking across all content

Content planning driven by search intent and keyword opportunity

Google Search Console — regular monitoring, tracking rankings, flagging drops, identifying quick wins

Google Analytics — understanding what content drives traffic and conversions

Technical SEO basics — site audits, crawl issues, page speed flags, schema where relevant

Local SEO — Google Business Profile optimisation, Iceland-specific search behaviour

AI search visibility — monitoring and improving YFIR's presence in ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini responses via Visby

Competitor content analysis — understanding what's ranking and why



Video

Edit short-form social video — Reels, TikToks, and Stories — from raw footage we supply

Create engaging edits with subtitles, music, hooks, and pacing suited to each platform

TikTok content should be native-feeling — not repurposed Instagram content

You won't be shooting — we supply the raw material, you make it work



Podcast (launching soon)

Edit and produce our podcast/chatshow — recorded conversations, interviews, Iceland stories

Handle audio cleanup, structure, chapter markers, and show notes

Publish and distribute via our podcast hosting platform

Repurpose episodes into social clips, TikToks, and newsletter content



Reporting

Monthly performance reporting via Reportz covering SEO, social, newsletter, and video metrics

Track hours per task area using Toggl

Flag what's working, what isn't, and what to do about it



The multiplier in practice: one recorded conversation becomes a podcast episode, TikTok clips, Instagram Reels, a newsletter feature, and an SEO-optimised blog post. That's the leverage this role creates.



The tools we use

Tool

Purpose

Claude

AI writing, research, content drafting, and repurposing

Followr

Social media scheduling and publishing across all platforms including TikTok

WordPress

Website content management

MailerLite

 newsletters

ClickRank

AI SEO automation and on-page optimisation

Visby

AI visibility tracking across ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini

Reportz

Marketing performance reporting and dashboards

Toggl

Time tracking per project and task area

Canva

Graphics and visual assets

Google GSC & GA

SEO monitoring and traffic analysis





What we're looking for

You are genuinely interested in Iceland.

Not 'Iceland seems nice' interested. Actually curious — about the history, the folklore, the landscapes, the culture, the food, the people. You don't need to have been here. But you need to care enough that writing about Iceland feels like a privilege, not a task. If Iceland is just a backdrop, this isn't the right fit.



Writing is the foundation.

You write clearly, warmly, and with personality. You can adapt tone — conversational for travel audiences, professional for B2B partners. You don't write like a chatbot and you don't write like a press release.



SEO is a genuine skill, not a checkbox.

You understand how search works — keyword intent, on-page structure, what makes content rank versus what makes it just exist. You write for humans first and search engines second, and you know how to do both simultaneously. Bonus if you understand GEO — how AI models like ChatGPT and Claude surface travel content.



AI fluency is non-negotiable.

We want someone who has genuinely built AI into their daily workflow — using it to research faster, draft quicker, find keyword opportunities, repurpose content smarter — while producing output that still sounds like a person wrote it. Show us how you work, not just that you use it.



Video editing is real, not decorative.

You know CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, Premiere, or equivalent. You understand what makes a TikTok stop a scroll in the first two seconds. Short-form travel content experience is a strong plus.



TikTok and Instagram are growth priorities.

Our main following lives on
 — over 37,000 followers. Now we want to grow. Instagram and TikTok are the two platforms where we're actively building presence, and this hire plays a central role in that.



You thrive wearing multiple hats.

On Monday you might be deep in keyword research. On Tuesday you're editing for social media. On Wednesday you're writing a newsletter. The right person finds that energising, not overwhelming.



You track your time without being asked.

We use Toggl to log hours per task area. This isn't about surveillance — it's about understanding where time goes so we can scope the role fairly and adjust over time if needed.



Podcast editing is a bonus, not a barrier.

If you've edited audio before — removed filler, cleaned levels, structured a conversation — great. If you're willing to learn, tell us.



Beyond the above:

Social media management across Instagram,
, LinkedIn, and TikTok

Comfortable with WordPress — publishing, formatting, images

Familiar with MailerLite or a comparable
 platform

Self-managed — you deliver without daily check-ins

English fluency required. Additional languages are a bonus

Travel, hospitality, or tourism experience is strongly preferred



The role

Type: Freelance / contractor, ongoing retainer

Hours: Approx. 80 hours/month (50% equivalent), tracked via Toggl

Location: Fully remote — anywhere with good English and reliable internet

Timezone: Flexible, with occasional overlap with GMT

Reporting to: Valur, CEO



How to apply

Skip the generic cover letter. Include these two things instead:



1. A writing sample.

Either something you've already written about travel, or a
word blog intro written for this application. Topic: 'Why Iceland in January is better than you think.' You can use AI — we'll judge the output, not whether you used it.



2. A short note on your AI workflow.

In
words: how do you use AI tools in your content, SEO, or video work? What tools, what for, and what do you still do yourself?



Bonus: If you have a video edit, TikTok portfolio, podcast clip, or SEO case study to share, include a link.



Applications without items 1 and 2 will not be reviewed.



Send your application to: 

Subject line: Content & Marketing - [Your Name]
salary: 12-22 USD per hour based on experience

title: Short Form Video Editor & Social Media Manager — UK Property Development
description:
About the role:



I am a UK based property developer documenting my journey building a property portfolio and working on real development projects. I need a skilled video editor and social media manager to take my raw iPhone footage and turn it into engaging short form reels posted consistently across Instagram, TikTok and YouTube Shorts.



Your main responsibilities:



Editing raw iPhone footage into short form reels and TikTok videos 30-90 seconds long. Adding captions, text overlays, transitions and music. Creating strong hooks in the first 3 seconds of every video. Writing captions and hashtags for each post. Scheduling and posting content daily. Organising raw footage in our shared Google Drive. Repurposing longer videos into multiple short clips. Telling me what footage you need so I just film and upload.



What I need from you:



Proven experience editing short form reels and TikTok content. Strong understanding of what performs well on Instagram and TikTok in 2025. Ability to work independently with minimal supervision. Good written English for captions. Reliable, consistent and communicative. Quick turnaround — ideally 24-48 hours per video.



The workflow:



I film on iPhone 16 Pro Max and upload to shared Google Drive organised by video number. You download, edit, caption, schedule and post. Simple and straightforward. Your job is to make my content look professional and grow my audience.



Please include in your application:



Examples of reels or TikTok videos you have edited. Your hourly rate. Your availability and turnaround time per video. Why you would be good for this role.



Hours: Flexible — approximately 10-15 hours per week to start with potential to grow.



Rate: Please state your expected hourly rate.



Niche: Property development and investment — UK market.

mbers

title: **AMAZING Media Buyer (Facebook Ads Specialist) Needed ASAP***
description:
We are an innovative marketing company headquartered in New York, known for accelerating growth for DTC brands through strategic solutions and data-driven optimizations. We are seeking an experienced Media Buyer with deep expertise in
 Ads to join our dynamic team. If you're passionate about driving results through paid media and have a proven track record in scaling campaigns profitably, we want to hear from you!



Role Overview:

As our Media Buyer, you will play a crucial role in planning, executing, and optimizing
 Ads campaigns for our clients. You’ll work closely with our creative, data, and account management teams to develop paid media strategies that align with client goals and deliver measurable results. Your expertise will ensure high-performing campaigns that drive customer acquisition, increase ROI, and maximize brand growth.



Key Responsibilities:



Manage and execute
 Ads campaigns across various 
 brands, optimizing performance to meet and exceed KPIs.

Analyze campaign data and metrics to identify trends, provide actionable insights, and adjust strategies in real-time to improve performance.

Develop audience targeting strategies to reach specific customer segments and maximize ad reach and relevance.

Collaborate with the creative team to develop compelling ad creatives, copy, and landing pages that drive conversions.

Monitor budgets, report on campaign performance, and make recommendations for improvement based on data analysis.

Implement A/B testing to optimize ads, landing pages, and other campaign elements.

Stay up-to-date with the latest trends, tools, and best practices in paid social advertising to ensure we are leveraging the most effective strategies.

Work closely with clients to understand their business objectives, providing them with insights and suggestions to improve their media buying strategy.

Requirements:



Proven experience in media buying with a strong focus on
 Ads, managing budgets across multiple accounts.

Deep understanding of
 Ads Manager, audience targeting, campaign setup, and optimization techniques.

Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret campaign performance data and make data-driven decisions.

Familiarity with other paid media platforms (Google Ads, TikTok Ads) is a plus.

Experience with A/B testing, creative optimization, and funnel building.

Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a focus on delivering results for clients.

Bonus Skills:



Experience in scaling campaigns for eCommerce and DTC brands.

Familiarity with third-party tools for automation, optimization, and reporting (e.g., Supermetrics, AdEspresso).

Knowledge of additional social media advertising platforms such as TikTok, Pinterest, and Snapchat.

Why Join Us?



Collaborate with a talented and driven team dedicated to achieving growth for DTC brands.

Flexibility to work remotely/hybrid, offering a great work-life balance.

Opportunity to work on exciting projects that challenge your creativity and analytical mindset.

Competitive salary and benefits package.

Play a key role in driving measurable success for top eCommerce brands.

If you're ready to take your media buying expertise to the next level and make a significant impact on the growth of DTC brands through paid media strategies, apply today!



We wish you all the best and we are looking towards your application...





Best Wishes,

M

title: Amazon Fba prodct researcher
description:
Product Researcher (Part-Time, Remote) — Vitamo

We're a growing Amazon FBA brand with a portfolio of supplements, skincare, and haircare products. We're hiring a part-time Product Researcher to start small and grow into a core member of the team over the next few years.

How this starts

3 hours per week. You'll be doing product research — identifying opportunities in our categories using Helium 10, analysing competition, search volume, and revenue potential, and delivering clean, structured outputs we can act on.

If the work is good, hours increase. Fast.

Where it goes

Within a few weeks, the role expands into supplier sourcing — contacting manufacturers in the USA, getting quotes, pushing products from idea to launch-ready. Eventually you'll own the full workflow from spotted opportunity to sourced product, with minimal hand-holding.

This is a ground-floor role with a real growth path for someone who wants to build deep knowledge of how a product brand operates.

What we're looking for



Comfortable working independently and taking initiative — you don't wait to be told what to do next

Organised and precise with data — outputs need to be clean, not approximate

Confident on the phone and in written communication with suppliers

Familiar with Helium 10 or willing to learn it quickly

Interested in e-commerce and physical products, not just looking for hours



What this is not

This is not a high-volume task role. We're looking for one person who thinks, not someone who fills in a spreadsheet on autopilot. If you find something interesting, we want to hear about it.

Details



Remote, flexible hours

3 hrs/week to start, increasing based on performance

Paid trial project before any commitment on either side



To apply, send a short note on why this interests you and any relevant experience. Please send C.V too and try avoid using AI to apply or at least use one that is good and doesnt sound like all the other applicants

title: AI Image and Video Creator (VEO3, Nano Banana Pro)

title: Community Growth Specialist — Reddit, Quora & English Forums
description:
We are a proxy service with 20M+ residential IPs, used by digital marketers, affiliate teams, and e-commerce businesses worldwide.



We're looking for someone to grow our brand across English-speaking communities by writing helpful, natural content — NOT ads or spam.



What you'll do:

- Write 20-30 helpful comments/answers per day on Reddit, Quora, and niche forums

- Find relevant threads where people ask about proxies, VPNs, anti-detect browsers, ad accounts, web scraping

- Manage a network of accounts across platforms (we provide the accounts)

- Build reputation and karma on these accounts over time

- Track your work and report weekly (threads, engagement, results)



You're a great fit if:

- Your English writing sounds native — not textbook, not robotic

- You've managed multiple accounts on Reddit, Quora, forums, or social media

- You understand how online communities work (what gets upvoted vs. what gets banned)

- You know the difference between being genuinely helpful and being a shill

- Bonus: you know what residential proxies, anti-detect browsers, or affiliate marketing are



This is NOT a content writing job. You will be a community participant — helping real people and naturally building brand awareness.



Details:

- Full-time (8 hours/day, flexible schedule)

- Long-term (6+ months)

- Salary: $
/month depending on experience

- Start: immediately



To apply: fill out this form — 



We will NOT consider applications without the form. It takes 15 minutes and helps us find the right person fast.

title: ???? Junior Media Buyer (Performance Marketing) – Remote | Fast-Growing E-Commerce Startup
description:
?? Only apply if you have hands-on experience with paid media platforms (Meta preferred) and understand performance marketing fundamentals such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, and attribution models.



Please apply here: 
 



About Skinnify

At Skinnify (www.skinnify.co), we’re reinventing fitness by integrating resistance into everyday clothing. In just three years, we’ve grown from 0 to 150,000+ customers, and we’re just getting started.

To get a better idea of our company and what it’s like to work on our team, watch this short video:



We are now looking for a Junior Media Buyer to join our performance marketing team and help manage and scale our paid media campaigns.

You will primarily assist with campaigns for our US store, where we invest over $500,000 per month in paid advertising. This is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience inside a fast-paced, high-growth e-commerce environment.



???? Your Responsibilities

? Assist in launching and managing paid campaigns across Meta, TikTok, AppLovin, Taboola, and other platforms

? Conduct A/B tests on creatives, audiences, funnels, and bidding strategies

? Support daily performance monitoring and reporting, using Triple Whale as the main source of truth

? Work closely with the creative and design teams to suggest improvements for ad creatives based on performance data

? Assist with basic conversion tracking and pixel setup when needed

? Monitor campaign metrics and identify opportunities to improve CPA, ROAS, and overall campaign efficiency

? Stay up to date with paid media trends, algorithm updates, and best practices



???? What We’re Looking For

?? 1+ year of experience in paid media buying or performance marketing

?? Hands-on experience with at least one major ad platform (Meta preferred)

?? Strong analytical skills and comfort working with campaign data

?? Basic understanding of key performance metrics such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, LTV

?? Familiarity with different attribution models

?? Strong attention to detail and ability to execute tasks quickly

?? Proactive mindset with a strong willingness to learn

?? Fluent in English



???? Compensation & Work Setup

???? $7 USD/hour

?? Hourly-based work with time tracking

???? Must have at least 4 hours overlap with UTC-3 working hours

???? Expected workload: 5–10 hours per week





???? Why Join Skinnify?

???? Remote Work – Work from anywhere in the world

???? High Growth Environment – Join a brand that grew from 0 to 150,000+ customers in 3 years

???? Massive Ad Budgets – Work with campaigns investing $500K+ per month

???? Hands-On Learning – Gain real experience in scaling performance marketing campaigns

???? Work With Experts – Collaborate with experienced media buyers, creatives, and strategists

? Great Team Culture – Check out our employee reviews on Glassdoor:





???????? The Skinnify Story

Growing up, our founder Christian wasn’t the easiest kid.

His mom gave everything to raise him and his siblings - often neglecting her own health in the process. As an adult, Christian wanted to help her become fitter, but she simply didn't have time for the gym.

The solution came during his own workout. While using resistance bands, he thought:

"What if these were built into leggings?"

That idea became our first product: Resistance Band Leggings that turn everyday movement into exercise.

Since then, we've expanded into sleeves, shoes, and more, with the mission of helping busy women become fitter without needing the gym.



???? Skinnify’s Core Values

???? Innovation – We design groundbreaking clothing with built-in resistance.

???? Effectiveness – Our products help people burn more calories and get fitter with every step.

???? Accessibility – Fitness should be possible for everyone, even the busiest people.

???? Fun – Exercise should feel effortless and enjoyable.

? Quality – We build durable, high-quality products that last.



???? Ready to Grow With Us?

If you're passionate about performance marketing and e-commerce, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Performance Video Editor – Meta Ads (DTC Brand, $150K+/mo Ad Spend)
description:
We're a US-based parenting education brand spending $200K+/month on Meta ads. We need a dedicated performance video editor who can produce 30–50+ ad variations per week from existing footage & sourced footage using CapCut.



This is NOT a creative/artistic editing role. This is high-volume, modular ad assembly — swapping hooks, bodies, CTAs, text overlays, and music to create dozens of structurally distinct ad variations from a library of raw footage components.



WHAT YOU'LL DO EVERY WEEK:

— Assemble 30–50+ Meta ad variations from modular footage (hooks, body clips, CTAs, B-roll)

— Add bold, scroll-stopping text overlays optimized for sound-off viewing (85% of Meta views are muted)

— Apply auto-captions with intentional styling (not default dumps)

— Swap hooks, CTAs, music, and pacing across variations

— Export in correct specs (9:16, 1:1, 4:5) with proper safe zones

— Organize and maintain our shared asset library

— Review ad performance data weekly and learn what's working (we share ROAS, CTR, hook rate, hold rate)



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

— 2+ years editing video ads specifically for Meta/Facebook/Instagram (not YouTube, not wedding, not film — META ADS)

— CapCut is your primary tool and you're fast in it

— You understand direct response: hooks, pattern interrupts, pacing, urgency, CTAs

— You can look at raw footage and see 10+ variations without being told

— You've worked with DTC or e-commerce brands before

— You understand that "ugly" authentic-looking ads outperform polished cinematic ads on Meta

— You're comfortable with repetitive, high-volume production work (this is assembly-line speed, not artisan craftsmanship)

— You can work independently from briefs with minimal revision cycles

— Reliable internet, own computer, available during US business hours overlap (at least 4 hours)



BONUS (not required):

— Experience with AI tools (Higgsfield, Kling, ElevenLabs) for lip sync or AI video

— Basic understanding of Meta Ads Manager (reading performance metrics)

— Experience adding film grain, compression artifacts, ambient audio to make ads feel organic



WHAT WE PROVIDE:

— Full organized asset library (hooks, bodies, CTAs, overlays all pre-sorted)

— Loom-based SOPs for every ad format we run

— Weekly performance data so you see which of YOUR edits are winning

— Reference ads and creative briefs for every batch

— Clear, fast feedback (no 5-day review cycles)

— Long-term role with growth path — editors who develop performance intuition get promoted to lead creative roles



HOW TO APPLY:

Do NOT just send your resume. To be considered, you MUST include:



1. A link to 3–5 Meta/Facebook/Instagram video ads you personally edited (not YouTube content, not reels for organic — PAID ADS). If they're in a portfolio, timestamp or label which ones are yours.



2. Answer this question in 2–3 sentences: "If an ad has a 50% hook rate but viewers drop off at the 5-second mark, what would you change in the edit?"



3. Your honest self-rating 1–10 on CapCut speed and proficiency.



4. Which brands have you worked with before in which role and at which ad spend levels?



Applications without all 3 items will be ignored. We review applications within 48 hours and top candidates will receive a PAID test task ($50 USD) using real footage from our brand.



This is a sensitive product category (child safety education) — the content is empowering and positive, not graphic. You should be comfortable editing content about keeping kids safe.

title: Creative Strategist & Video Editor – Meta Ads (DTC Parenting Brand, $150K+/mo Ad Spend)
description:
TYPE OF WORK: Full Time

SALARY: $800–$1,600/month depending on experience

HOURS PER WEEK: 40

THE ROLE

We're a US-based parenting education brand spending $150K+/month on Meta ads, serving 26,000+ families across multiple product lines. We need someone who can do two things most people can't do at once: think strategically about what ads to make AND produce them fast in CapCut.

This is not just an editing role. You'll study our ad data, mine customer comments for hooks, analyze what's working and what's fatiguing, come up with new concepts, AND cut the videos yourself. You're the person who wakes up thinking "that hook is dying, here's what we should test next" and then actually builds it.

If you're a video editor who's been itching to move into creative strategy but nobody's given you the shot — this is that shot.

WHAT YOUR WEEK LOOKS LIKE

Strategy & Research (30% of your time):



Review ad performance weekly: hook rates, hold rates, CTR, ROAS. Know which ads are winning, which are fatiguing, and why.

Mine our 
ments, customer reviews, and DMs for language and hooks that real parents use. Surface the gold so we can turn it into ads.

Study competitor ads in the Meta Ad Library. Flag creative approaches worth testing.

Write concepts and scripts for new ads. Not full creative briefs — short, clear outlines: here's the hook, here's the angle, here's why I think it'll work.

Propose 5–10 new ad concepts per week with a point of view on each one.



Production & Editing (70% of your time):



Produce 20–30+ ad variations per week from existing footage, sourced footage, and AI tools in CapCut.

Assemble modular ads: swapping hooks, bodies, CTAs, text overlays, and music to create structurally distinct variations.

Add bold, scroll-stopping text overlays optimized for sound-off viewing.

Apply auto-captions with intentional styling (not default dumps).

Export in correct specs (9:16, 1:1, 4:5) with proper safe zones.

Organize and maintain our shared creative asset library.



WHO YOU ARE



You have 1–3 years editing video ads for Meta/Facebook/Instagram (not YouTube, not wedding videos, not film). Even 6 months is fine if your work is sharp.

CapCut is your primary tool and you're fast in it.

You understand direct response advertising: hooks, pattern interrupts, pacing, urgency, CTAs. You know that "ugly" authentic-looking ads outperform polished cinematic content on Meta.

You have genuine curiosity about WHY certain ads work. You don't just follow briefs — you have opinions about what to test next.

You can look at a set of performance data and draw conclusions without being told what to look for.

You're organized, self-directed, and can manage your own time between strategy work and production.

You're comfortable with high-volume output. Some weeks are more thinking, some weeks are more cutting, but the pace is always fast.

Reliable internet, own computer, available during US business hours overlap (at least 4 hours).



BONUS (NOT REQUIRED)



Experience with AI video tools (Creatify, HeyGen, Kling, ElevenLabs) for lip sync or AI-generated video ads.

Experience working with DTC or e-commerce brands.

Basic understanding of Meta Ads Manager (reading performance metrics, not managing campaigns).

Experience adding film grain, compression artifacts, ambient audio to make ads feel organic.



WHAT WE PROVIDE



Full organized asset library (hooks, bodies, CTAs, overlays all pre-sorted).

Loom-based SOPs for every ad format we run.

Weekly performance data so you can see which of YOUR concepts and edits are winning.

A founder who is deeply involved in creative strategy and will work alongside you, not just hand you tasks. You'll learn fast here.

Clear, fast feedback. No 5-day review cycles.

Long-term role with real growth. This position grows with you — the better your strategic instincts get, the more ownership you take on.



HOW TO APPLY

Do NOT just send your resume. To be considered, you MUST include all of the following:



A link to 3–5 Meta/Facebook/Instagram video ads you personally edited (not YouTube content, not organic reels — PAID ADS). If they're in a portfolio, label which ones are yours.

Pick any DTC brand's
 page. Find one ad in their Meta Ad Library that you think is underperforming and explain in 3–4 sentences what you'd change and why. (This tells us how you think, not just how you edit.)

Answer this: "If an ad has a 50% hook rate but viewers drop off at the 5-second mark, what would you change in the edit?"

Your honest self-rating 1–10 on CapCut speed and proficiency.

A short Loom (under 3 minutes) introducing yourself and showing us one project you're proud of. This isn't about production quality — we want to hear how you think and communicate.



Applications missing any of these items will be skipped. We review within 48 hours and top candidates receive a PAID test task ($50 USD) using real footage from our brand.

Note: This is a sensitive product category (child safety education and emotional regulation for kids). The content is empowering and positive, not graphic. You should be comfortable editing content about keeping kids safe and helping families.

title: Proactive E-commerce & Admin Assistant (Part-Time / High Autonomy)
description:
About the Role:



I am a busy entrepreneur running a car dealership alongside an active e-commerce business in Singapore. I do not have the time to micromanage. I am looking for a highly organized, independent, and proactive Virtual Assistant to take complete ownership of my daily task checklists.



You must be the kind of person who solves problems before I even notice them, suggests improvements to my systems, and can be trusted to manage the daily workflow flawlessly.



Schedule & Compensation:



Rate: $5.50 / hour (Approx. $500 / month)



Hours: 3 hours per day.



Schedule: 6 days a week on a fixed daily schedule (exact working hours are open for discussion).



Opportunity for Extra Pay: If you want to work 7 days a week to earn extra money, that option is always open. We can discuss this during the interview.



Core Responsibilities:



E-Commerce Daily Operations (Shopee/Lazada/1688): Fully manage the daily health and operations of multiple e-commerce stores. This includes executing customer service, processing specific pre-orders and 1688 orders, managing returns/refunds, monitoring inventory thresholds, entering platform campaigns, and actively checking for banned or deleted listings.



Automotive Business Admin (Fitz Automobile): Maintain back-end organization for a car dealership. This involves syncing vehicle prices across multiple listing platforms, diligently tracking and alerting me of road tax expirations, and organizing incoming invoices and new car data into Google Sheets.



Requirements



(Optional) Proven experience in E-commerce management (specifically Shopee/Lazada Seller Centers) and general administrative support.



Extreme attention to detail. you execute daily checklists with zero missed steps.



High level of autonomy; you know how to find answers and troubleshoot issues independently without waiting for instructions.



Strong organizational skills and the ability to work highly efficiently within a 3-hour daily window.



Excellent English communication skills (written and spoken).



How to Apply (READ CAREFULLY):



If you do not follow these instructions exactly, your application will be automatically deleted.



Subject Line: Make the subject line of your application: "Proactive VA: [Your Favorite Color] - [Your Name]"



Video Introduction: Record a 1-to-2-minute video introducing yourself. In the video, explain why you are a good fit for a highly autonomous role and give one example of a time you proactively solved a problem for a past client without being asked.



Written Question: In your application message, answer this briefly: If a product listing gets unexpectedly deleted by Lazada and I am unreachable, what are your immediate next steps?

You’ll be working closely with our founders and team to deliver measurable results across multiple client accounts.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Manage and optimise Meta 
 & Instagram) ad campaigns

THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR:

People who can’t show past results



IDEAL CANDIDATE

SUCCESS IN THIS ROLE LOOKS LIKE:

title: Bookkeeper  (E-commerce | Full-Time | Remote)
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing 7-figure e-commerce dropshipping company, primarily operating on Shopify. We work with multiple suppliers and vendors, managing high daily order volumes and transactions across different systems.Our business is fast-paced, detail-driven, and systems-focused. We value accuracy, consistency, and people who take ownership of their work.



The Role



We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented  Bookkeeper to support our day-to-day financial operations.

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role where your primary responsibility will be to ensure all financial transactions are accurately recorded, tracked, and reconciled. 



Key Responsibilities

1. Accounts Payable & Receivable

Process and track vendor payments via bank and dealer portals

Ensure all payments are completed accurately and on time

Monitor incoming payments from customers (wire transfers)

Maintain clear records of all receivables and payables



2. Reconciliation & Daily Financial Checks

Reconcile Shopify payouts

Identify and flag any discrepancies or missing payments



3. Vendor & Supplier Management

Process and track supplier credits and refunds

Reconcile vendor statements and maintain accurate credit balances

Follow up on missing credits or discrepancies when needed



4. Bookkeeping & Data Entry

Input Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)



Requirements

2–3 minimum years experience in bookkeeping or finance support

Experience with e-commerce businesses (Shopify preferred)

Familiarity with accounting tools like Xero, QuickBooks, or similar

Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy

Comfortable working with spreadsheets (Google Sheets / Excel)

Good written and verbal English communication





What We’re Looking For

Highly detail-oriented — you catch small errors others miss

Organized and consistent in your work

Reliable and able to follow processes daily

Proactive in flagging issues or discrepancies

Comfortable working in a structured, fast-moving environment



What’s in it for You?

Fully remote, stable full-time role

 Opportunity to grow within a scaling e-commerce business

Learn and develop within a systems-driven environment

Work with an international team



Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (8 hours/day)

Individual Performance Bonuses

title: Content Creator, Video Editor & Social Media Manager
description:
**About us:**



Modern Menopause is a Canadian virtual healthcare platform focused on evidence-based menopause care. We support women through perimenopause, menopause, and post-menopause with personalized treatment options, including hormone replacement therapy (HRT), non-hormonal therapies, and virtual care from licensed nurse practitioners and physicians.



We are building a trusted, modern brand in women’s health and are looking for a creative, detail-oriented **Content Creator/Video Editor & Social Media Manager** to help bring that vision to life across digital channels.



**Role Overview:**



We’re looking for someone who can blend **content creation, video editing, graphic design, and social media execution** into one cohesive role.



You will create educational and brand-forward content focused on menopause, perimenopause, HRT, symptoms, treatment options, and women’s health in midlife. You should be comfortable working with medically relevant topics, translating clinical information into clear consumer-facing content, and ensuring content remains accurate, credible, and easy to understand. This includes designing static graphics, editing short-form videos, working with in-house footage, adding B-roll and branded visual elements, and posting or scheduling content across our social channels.



This is a freelance role with the potential to grow into a longer-term part-time or full-time opportunity based on fit and business needs. The role may also expand to support other health-related brands under our umbrella.



**What You’ll Do:**



- Create social media content for Modern Menopause across Instagram,
, LinkedIn, YouTube, and other relevant platforms

- Design static content such as carousel posts, educational graphics, quote posts, infographics, and promotional assets

- Edit short-form video content for social media using in-house footage, captions, transitions, B-roll, and other enhancements

- Turn clinical or educational information into content that is clear, engaging, and aligned with the brand

- Write or adapt captions and on-screen copy for social posts and video content

- Schedule and publish content based on a content calendar

- Maintain visual consistency across channels and ensure content aligns with our tone, messaging, and brand identity

- Collaborate with our team to brainstorm new content ideas, campaigns, and educational series

- Repurpose long-form or existing content into social-first formats

- Support content creation for other wellness or health brands under the company when needed



**What We’re Looking For:**



- Experience in **content creation, social media management, graphic design, and video editing**

- Strong portfolio showing social media content, branded design work, and short-form video editing

- Ability to create content and visuals that feels modern, polished, and credible in a healthcare or wellness setting

- Experience editing Instagram Reels, short-form YouTube videos, and other vertical video formats

- Proficiency in tools such as **Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, Premiere Pro, CapCut, Final Cut Pro**, or similar platforms

- Strong understanding of social media best practices, including platform-specific formatting and trends

- Strong visual taste and ability to create clean, elevated, on-brand assets

- Excellent written English and attention to detail

- Comfortable taking clinical or health-related input and translating it into accessible content

- Strong organization and ability to work independently in a remote environment

title: Tech-Savvy Online Business Manager (OBM)|Exec VA
description:
She Who Scales is a coaching business helping female entrepreneurs step into leadership, visibility, and scalable income through identity work, messaging, and business systems.



We are hiring a tech-savvy, execution-focused Online Business Manager (OBM) who can take ideas, offers, and frameworks and turn them into fully built, functional backend systems that generate revenue.



This role is for someone who is both strategic enough to see the big picture and hands-on enough to build inside platforms like GoHighLevel and Kajabi.



Tech-Savvy Online Business Manager (OBM) – She Who Scales

Overview



She Who Scales is a coaching business helping female entrepreneurs step into leadership, visibility, and scalable income through identity work, messaging, and business systems.



We are hiring a tech-savvy, execution-focused Online Business Manager (OBM) who can take ideas, offers, and frameworks and turn them into fully built, functional backend systems that generate revenue.



This role is for someone who is both strategic enough to see the big picture and hands-on enough to build inside platforms like GoHighLevel and Kajabi.



The Role



You will act as the operational backbone of the business.



The founder brings vision, content, and direction.

You translate that into systems, funnels, and launches that are executed quickly and efficiently.



You are not here to wait for step-by-step instructions. You are here to take ownership, problem-solve, and build.



Core Responsibilities:



1. Funnel & Backend Build (Primary Focus)

Build and manage funnels inside GoHighLevel, Kajabi, or similar platforms

Set up landing pages, checkout flows, and product delivery

Connect systems end-to-end (payments,
s, automations)

Build and implement ManyChat flows for lead generation and sales

Ensure all systems are functional, tested, and optimized



2. Offer Implementation

Take raw offers, drafts, and frameworks and structure them into clear products

Upload and organize courses, programs, and digital products

Build onboarding flows and client access systems

Turn ideas into live, sellable assets



3. Project & Launch Execution

Turn ideas into clear execution plans with timelines and deliverables

Lead launches of offers, funnels, and products

Ensure all moving parts are completed on time

Identify blockers and solve them quickly



4. Systems & Operations

Build and maintain backend systems (Notion, Google Drive, CRM tools)

Create SOPs for repeatable processes

Streamline workflows across content, sales, and delivery

Improve efficiency and eliminate bottlenecks



5. Content & Funnel Alignment

Ensure content connects to offers and funnels

Support implementation of lead generation systems

Coordinate scheduling and backend content workflows



6. Performance Tracking

Track funnel performance (conversion rates, drop-offs, sales)

Provide weekly insights and recommendations

Identify areas for optimization



First 30 Days (Expected Outcomes):



Audit and organize existing offers, drafts, and assets

Identify and prioritize one core offer for launch

Build and launch a low-ticket funnel

Set up ManyChat automation connected to the funnel

Establish backend systems for ongoing execution





Required Skills & Experience:



Proven experience building funnels inside GoHighLevel, Kajabi, or similar platforms

Strong understanding of online coaching businesses and digital products

Experience with ManyChat,
 marketing systems, and automations

Ability to build systems from incomplete or messy inputs

Strong technical and problem-solving skills

Ability to execute quickly without constant supervision



Ideal Candidate:



Hands-on and execution-driven, not just a manager

Comfortable working with evolving ideas and direction

Fast, resourceful, and solution-oriented

Understands how backend systems support sales and growth

Takes ownership and moves projects forward independently



What This Role Is NOT:



Not a content creator or brand strategist

Not a passive assistant waiting for tasks

Not focused on paid ads or ad management



Working Style:



You take initiative and ownership of projects

You prioritize execution over perfection

You communicate clearly and proactively

You bring solutions, not just questions



Goal of This Role:



To build a structured, scalable backend for She Who Scales, where offers, funnels, and systems are fully operational, allowing the founder to focus on content, visibility, and sales.

title: Voice UGC Video Editor — Nightlife Conten
description:
I'm looking for a content creator to produce 20 short-form videos per week for a nightlife brand. This is a fully remote ongoing monthly role. You will research nightlife content relevant to the Dallas bar and club scene, edit it into engaging short-form videos, and record a conversational voiceover for each piece.



All voiceovers must be recorded in a clear American English accent. The tone should feel like a friend giving a recommendation — casual, warm, and genuine. Not a script, not a commercial. AI voices are not allowed and will be flagged for termination.



All videos delivered in vertical 9:16 format optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. Ideal length 15–60 seconds per piece.



To apply please include: 2–3 examples of short-form videos with your voiceover, a 20–30 second voice sample, and your monthly rate for 80 pieces per month.



Compensation is open based on experience and quality. Ongoing arrangement with room to grow. All content becomes brand property upon delivery. Will pay bonuses for videos that reach over 300k views.

/Instagram) ad campaigns end-to-end

s and questions

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 2+ years managing paid ad campaigns on Meta Ads Manager (this is the most important skill)

- Proven track record of running profitable campaigns for digital products, courses, or e-commerce

- Experience with Google Ads (Search and YouTube campaigns)

- Strong understanding of conversion tracking, pixel setup, and attribution

- Comfortable reading ad dashboards and making data-driven optimization decisions

- Experience with audience building: lookalikes, retargeting, interest-based targeting

- Good written English — you may need to tweak ad copy and respond to customers

- Self-starter who can manage campaigns independently without daily direction

- Reliable internet connection



NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with TikTok Ads Manager

- Familiarity with Kajabi (or similar course platforms like Teachable, Thinkific)

- Experience with ScoreApp, Zapier, or similar automation tools

- Background in marketing online courses or digital info products

- Basic understanding of funnel strategy (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU)



HOW TO APPLY

Send your application with:

- Screenshots or case studies showing ad campaign results you have managed (ROAS, CPA, or revenue metrics preferred)

- A short message explaining which ad platforms you are strongest on and the types of products you have promoted

- Your expected monthly salary in USD



Applications without proof of ad management experience will not be reviewed.

title: Product/Creatives Researcher
description:
We’re looking for a high-level product researcher who can consistently bring in winning products, competitor insights, and ready-to-run creatives for EU meta advertising. Your job is simple: find early-stage winning products (not saturated, not branded), break down competitor ads (Meta/shopify/funnels online), and deliver full product packs with angles, creatives, and deep reasoning on why it will sell.



 You MUST also source ads DAILY — minimum 10+ for each product (videos + images), and go 5x deeper when needed so we always have enough creatives to test aggressively. This is NOT basic research — you need to understand what makes a product convert, validate demand with real data  longevity/reach etc., and think like an operator, not a VA, since strong researchers are expected to analyze trends, competitors, and positioning before anything gets tested . 



We don’t want random product lists — we want actionable insights we can launch immediately in our EU stores. Must have done job before and understanding in product research (beauty/health/welness) only, strong understanding of ad libraries + creatives, and ability to work independently without hand-holding. To apply, send 1 product with full breakdown (why it wins, audience, ads, angle, supplier) — who doesn't mention avocado will get ignored.



Looking forward to have you in our team !



Regards

title: Social Media Specialist
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



About Us:

CozifyHome is a dynamic and growing e-commerce brand. We are looking for a creative and data-driven individual to join our team and take our online presence to the next level.



Job Overview:



We are seeking a versatile Social Media Specialist to manage our digital storefront and social media landscapes. You will be the voice of our brand across Facebook, Tiktok and Instagram, responsible for building engaged communities and driving sales. Your role is crucial in bridging creative social content with our e-commerce platform, Shopify, to create a seamless customer journey from discovery to purchase.



Key Responsibilities:

- Social Media Management (Facebook, TikTok & Instagram):

- Develop and execute a cohesive social media strategy to increase brand awareness, engagement, and conversions.

- Create, curate, and publish high-quality, original content (including graphics, photos, videos, and Reels) daily.

- Manage all community interactions—responding to comments and messages in a timely and brand-appropriate manner.

- Plan and manage social media advertising campaigns on Facebook and Instagram to promote products, grow our audience, and achieve specific KPIs.

- Monitor social media trends, tools, and applications and recommend strategies for adoption.

- Collaborate on the creation of marketing assets (e.g., email banners, product graphics) that align with the brand identity.



Analytics & Reporting:

-Track, analyze, and report on social media performance (engagement, reach, follower growth) and e-commerce metrics (traffic, conversion rate).



Qualifications & Required Experience:

- A minimum of 1 year of professional experience in a similar role, managing social media activities.

- Demonstrable experience creating engaging content and growing a community on Facebook and Instagram.

- Portfolio or examples of social media you have created and managed.

- Proficiency with graphic design tools (e.g., Canva, Adobe Creative Suite) for creating social media assets.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a keen eye for detail.

- Ability to work independently, manage multiple projects, and meet deadlines.



Preferred Skills (Bonus Points):

- Experience with social media scheduling and analytics tools (e.g., Meta Business Suite, Hootsuite, Buffer, Later).

- Basic knowledge of SEO principles for content and product pages.

- Understanding of paid social advertising (Facebook/Instagram Ads Manager).



What We Offer:

- A creative and collaborative work environment.

- The opportunity to make a significant impact on a growing brand.



How to Apply:

If you are a creative and analytical marketer who lives and breathes social media and e-commerce, we would love to hear from you! Please submit your resume, a link to your portfolio or relevant work samples, and a brief cover letter explaining why you are the perfect fit for this role.

title: Experienced manual accessibility tester needed
description:
My name is Helen and  I'm looking for an experienced manual accessibility tester to work with me on a freelance, per-audit basis.



Here's how the work is structured. I run an automated pipeline before any manual testing begins. It covers around 13 WCAG 2.2 criteria and pre-populates a custom audit tool with results, screenshots, and evidence. What I need from you is everything that comes after that.



Your work splits into two areas:



1. Spot-check verification — reviewing criteria the automation has flagged as passed, and confirming those calls are correct using NVDA, browser DevTools, BrowserStack, Stark, and physical device testing where relevant.



2. Manual completion — testing the remaining WCAG 2.2 A and AA criteria that automation cannot reliably cover. You will document your findings directly in the audit tool I provide, following the same evidence format the automation uses.



You'll receive the pre-populated audit tool, a JSON results file, and the list of URLs tested. All the context you need to get started will be with those files. Shared BrowserStack access will be provided where required.



What I'm looking for:

- Solid WCAG 2.2 A and AA knowledge at criterion level

- Genuine NVDA experience, not just familiarity

- Comfortable with BrowserStack, Stark, and browser DevTools

- Able to work from a structured brief without needing a lot of back and forth

- Available for roughly 4 audits per month on a consistent basis



I'm based in Bali, so timezone-wise we're close, which makes communication straightforward.



If this sounds like a good fit, please share experience you have, your availability, day rate,
 address, and WhatsApp number and we can take it from there.

title: Commercial Operations & Systems Coordinator – Water Storage Tank Projects
description:
Raven Tanks is seeking a sharp, systems minded operator to help improve how our business runs across sales, quoting, and operations.



This is not a standard admin or estimating role. We are looking for someone highly intelligent who can think critically, build and improve workflows, and help connect our systems so the business runs faster and cleaner.



We currently run our pipeline through Pipedrive, and a big part of this role is improving how that system works in practice. This includes tightening up deal flow, follow up, handovers between sales and operations, and building automations to reduce manual work.



You will work directly with the leadership team to streamline processes, improve visibility across the pipeline, and support quoting and project coordination where required.



What you’ll do:



Review how work currently flows across sales, quoting, and operations

Identify gaps, inefficiencies, and areas for improvement

Build and improve workflows within Pipedrive and across our internal systems

Set up and manage automations (Zapier or similar) to reduce manual work

Improve follow up systems so deals don’t get lost or delayed

Support quoting by structuring information, templates, and outputs

Assist with project coordination tasks such as packing lists, job prep, and handovers

Maintain clean, structured data across our systems

Work closely with the team to make processes faster, clearer, and more reliable



We’re looking for:



Exceptional English communication, both written and spoken

Strong logical thinking and problem solving ability

Someone who naturally looks for better, faster ways of doing things

Comfortable working with data, systems, and structured workflows

Experience with Pipedrive or similar CRM systems

Experience with automation tools such as Zapier, Make, or similar

Some exposure to sales or sales support, with an understanding of how deals move through a pipeline

High attention to detail and strong organisational skills

Ability to take ownership and improve things without being told step by step

Reliable internet and consistent availability aligned with AEST business hours



This role suits someone who enjoys systems, structure, and problem solving. You should be comfortable working independently, taking ownership of tasks, and improving how things are done across the business.



We are not looking for someone to just follow instructions. We are looking for someone who can think, build, and improve.



Important:



Do not send a resume.



To apply, send a one minute whatsApp video to Matt on 
including all of the below.



Start your message with the word RAVEN.

title: Procurement & Systems Coordinator – Automation & Costing

What you’ll do:
